眼鏡.mnemonics: Sounds like 'May-gah-neh.' Picture someone in May going 'Neh' because they can't see without their glasses.

歌.mnemonics: Sounds like 'ooh-tah.' Imagine saying 'ooh' in wonder when you hear a beautiful song.

車.mnemonics: Sounds like 'cool-ma.' Imagine a really cool mom driving a fancy car.

長い.mnemonics: Sounds like 'nag-eye.' Think of a long, nagging sigh.

短い.mnemonics: Sounds like 'me-gee-guy.' Imagine a short guy who always says 'Me? Gee, I'm short.'

速い.mnemonics: Sounds like 'high-yai.' Think of something moving so fast it goes high and yells 'Yai!'

背が高い.mnemonics: Sounds like 'sega-ta-kai.' Picture a tall person who loves playing Sega games.

背が低い.mnemonics: Sounds like 'sega-hikui.' Imagine a short person who feels low (hikui) when they can't reach the Sega console.

頭がいい.mnemonics: Sounds like 'atama guy E.' Picture a smart guy named E who always uses his head (atama).

可愛い.mnemonics: Sounds like 'kawaii.' This is similar to the English word 'cawaii' for cute.

親切.mnemonics: Sounds like 'shin-sets.' Picture a kind person who always sets their heart (shin) on doing good.

便利.mnemonics: Sounds like 'ben-ri.' Imagine Ben, who is always handy and convenient to have around.

歌う.mnemonics: Sounds like 'ooh-ta-oo.' Picture singing a song with 'ooh' sounds.

被る.mnemonics: Sounds like 'cab-ru.' Imagine putting on a hat before getting into a cab.

english: to put on items below your waist
履く.mnemonics: Sounds like 'huck.' Think of 'hucking' on your pants or shoes.

知る.mnemonics: Sounds like 'sheer.' Picture getting to know something as clearly as seeing through sheer fabric.

知っています.mnemonics: Sounds like 'sheet-tea-mass.' Imagine confidently saying 'I know' as you sip a cup of tea.

知りません.mnemonics: Sounds like 'sheer-ma-sen.' Picture saying 'I don't know' with a sheer look of confusion.

住む.mnemonics: Sounds like 'sum.' Think of where you live as the sum of your home life.
